title: The Massage

jokes.text: The problem, quite honestly, has been you, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to quit the show due to exhaustion and headaches
jokes.punchline: Jeff bluntly tells him he's the problem, not the show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff characterization - direct, brutal honesty delivered matter-of-factly. Sets up Larry as his own worst enemy.

jokes.text: No, not Fox, I don't like their politics.
jokes.setup: Discussing network options for their show
jokes.punchline: Larry makes an arbitrary political judgment about Fox
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - making everything about his personal principles, even business decisions.

jokes.text: Don't stare, come on. Oh, my God, look at that.
jokes.setup: Jeff tells Larry not to stare at something
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by gawking
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective hypocrisy joke with tight timing.

jokes.text: It's called 'scarlet-letter punishment.'
jokes.setup: Seeing someone being publicly shamed
jokes.punchline: Literary reference to public humiliation
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that contextualizes modern public shaming.

jokes.text: He wanted to do a stop-and-chat. I didn't want to do a stop-and-chat.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks why Larry didn't say hello to someone he knows
jokes.punchline: Larry's invented terminology for casual social interaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry-ism. Creates a new phrase that perfectly captures his social anxiety while being instantly quotable.

jokes.text: You always have rules and phrasings, 'stop-and-chat.'
jokes.setup: Larry uses his invented terminology
jokes.punchline: Jeff calls out Larry's habit of creating social rules and phrases
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on Larry's character - Jeff recognizing Larry's pattern of systematizing social interactions.

jokes.text: No. Really? Surprise, surprise. That shocks me.
jokes.setup: Larry is asked to help stuff envelopes and says no
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reaction to Larry's predictable selfishness
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat - everyone knows Larry won't help with menial tasks.

jokes.text: Kim nails or Kim psychic?
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentions seeing Kim
jokes.punchline: Larry's need to clarify which service provider Kim is
jokes.explanation: Economical joke revealing the mundane reality of having multiple service providers with the same name.

jokes.text: Only you could have a bad time in Hawaii.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions having a bad time in Hawaii as predicted
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out how uniquely Larry this is
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation - captures Larry's ability to find misery anywhere.

jokes.text: Yeah, anybody could've predicted that.
jokes.setup: Jeff says only Larry could have a bad time in Hawaii
jokes.punchline: Larry deflects by claiming it was predictable, missing the point
jokes.explanation: Larry's defensive response completely misses Jeff's point about his unique neuroses.

jokes.text: Who's gonna pay to hear a bunch of bad shit? Nobody want to hear that.
jokes.setup: Discussion about psychics holding back bad news
jokes.punchline: Blunt economic reality of the psychic business
jokes.explanation: Cynical but accurate observation about the psychology of fortune telling.

jokes.text: They can't feel your tension, okay? You're just all wired up.
jokes.setup: Larry is frustrated with slow golfers
jokes.punchline: Jeff's oblivious advice while Larry is clearly agitated
jokes.explanation: Jeff's clueless attempt to calm Larry down while being completely wrong about the situation.

jokes.text: I can't hit in front of an audience. You know, I just can't.
jokes.setup: Larry is about to hit his golf shot
jokes.punchline: Reveals his performance anxiety about a casual golf game
jokes.explanation: Larry's social anxiety extending to recreational activities with strangers.

jokes.text: My mother did throw me against a brick wall when I was an infant. Maybe that had--
jokes.setup: Masseuse asks about childhood trauma affecting his body tension
jokes.punchline: Larry casually mentions extreme child abuse
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark comedy delivered with Larry's characteristic matter-of-fact tone. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I didn't get hurt at all.
jokes.setup: Larry mentioned being thrown against a brick wall as an infant
jokes.punchline: His bizarre reassurance that he wasn't injured
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that makes the abuse story even more absurd with his casual dismissal.

jokes.text: Yeah, she said I was a little prick.
jokes.setup: Asked if he talked to his mother about throwing him against a wall
jokes.punchline: His mother's dismissive and cruel response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that explains Larry's neuroses through his mother's complete lack of remorse. Perfectly delivered punchline.

jokes.text: It's good, it's delicious chi.
jokes.setup: Masseuse asks about his chi
jokes.punchline: Larry treats spiritual energy like food quality
jokes.explanation: Larry's literal-minded response to New Age concepts, treating chi like a menu item.

jokes.text: Would you like me to finish you off?
jokes.setup: End of massage session
jokes.punchline: Innocent question with sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for the main plot - the ambiguous phrasing that leads to Larry's predicament.

jokes.text: Stop. Stop? Stop.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes what's happening during the massage
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to stop the situation
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - Larry's delayed realization and awkward attempt to halt proceedings.

jokes.text: What in the God's name is wrong with you? You're nuts.
jokes.setup: Larry approaches Mike to tell his story
jokes.punchline: Mike's immediate aggressive reaction
jokes.explanation: Strong reaction establishing Mike's incredulity at Larry's situation.

jokes.text: I don't know what the hell she's talking about! I think she'll work more on my neck and that'll be it!
jokes.setup: Larry explains his confusion about the masseuse's offer
jokes.punchline: His innocent interpretation of 'finish you off'
jokes.explanation: Larry's genuine cluelessness about sexual euphemisms makes the situation both funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, are you messing around with me? Is this a gag? 'Cause my character goes to a masseuse who wants to give him a happy ending.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Mike about the massage incident
jokes.punchline: Mike thinks it's a joke because it matches his TV show plot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where life imitates art so closely it seems fake. Perfect Curb Your Enthusiasm self-reference.

jokes.text: You don't watch my show? You don't?
jokes.setup: Larry admits he doesn't watch Mike's show
jokes.punchline: Mike's wounded ego about Larry not watching
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment revealing Hollywood social dynamics and hurt feelings.

jokes.text: What do you mean? Who stops a whack-off?
jokes.setup: Larry says he stopped the massage
jokes.punchline: Mike's incredulous response about stopping mid-act
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective joke about male psychology and self-control.

jokes.text: You either do zero pumps or a thousand pumps.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry stopping at 12 pumps
jokes.punchline: Mike's all-or-nothing philosophy
jokes.explanation: Absurd but memorable rule about sexual encounters. The extreme numbers make it funnier.

jokes.text: If I stopped at 12 pumps, I would tell my wife. I'd go home and say, 'Honey, I was getting a massage and I took 12 pumps and I stopped 'cause I love you.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry's 12-pump stopping point
jokes.punchline: Mike's hypothetical confession to his wife
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific scenario that's both absurd and oddly logical. The exact number and romantic justification is perfect.

jokes.text: And if your wife ever finds out, you're a noble man, you made it stop.
jokes.setup: Mike discusses Larry's situation with stopping
jokes.punchline: Frames Larry's awkward stopping as heroic
jokes.explanation: Ironic reframing of Larry's embarrassing situation as noble behavior.

jokes.text: I ran into him on the street the other day and I didn't do a stop-and-chat.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions seeing Rick Crane
jokes.punchline: Returns to his invented 'stop-and-chat' terminology
jokes.explanation: Effective callback to earlier joke, reinforcing Larry's systematic approach to social interaction.

jokes.text: No means no!
jokes.setup: Larry tries to initiate sex when Cheryl wants to leave
jokes.punchline: Using consent language in a domestic comedy context
jokes.explanation: Appropriates serious consent language for marital comedy, walking the line between funny and problematic.

jokes.text: I've got a lump. I'm afraid she's gonna tell you about it! Do you hear what I'm saying? I'm dying!
jokes.setup: Larry desperately tries to stop Cheryl from seeing the psychic
jokes.punchline: Invents increasingly dramatic health scares
jokes.explanation: Larry's desperate escalation from lump to death shows his panic about the psychic discovering his secret.

jokes.text: Yeah, he having sex with her hand! She pump 32 times, then....
jokes.setup: Psychic describes Larry's massage to Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Specific and graphic details from the psychic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the psychic somehow knows the exact number of pumps, making her abilities seem real and terrifying.

jokes.text: She said that you're having an affair with a woman with long, red hair and a tattoo of a star.
jokes.setup: Cheryl confronts Larry about the psychic's revelation
jokes.punchline: Specific but completely wrong details about an affair
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - the psychic gets the massage right but completely wrong about an affair.

jokes.text: Maybe she's an anti-Semite. I'm sure there's some Korean anti-Semites.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain why the psychic would lie
jokes.punchline: Bizarre ethnic prejudice theory
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry paranoia - when caught in a lie, blame ethnic prejudice. Absurd but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Because if I just told you the truth about that, it means that I'm just a truthful person who would tell you the truth about anything.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks how she knows Larry isn't lying about the affair
jokes.punchline: Larry's convoluted logic about his honesty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic that somehow makes sense in Larry's mind. Classic Curb reasoning that's both absurd and oddly logical.

jokes.text: I'm not a cool guy. You gotta be cool to do that. I couldn't do that. I won't know how, what would I do?
jokes.setup: Larry denying he's having an affair
jokes.punchline: His self-awareness about lacking the skills for an affair
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-awareness from Larry - he knows he lacks the social skills for infidelity. Both funny and touching.

jokes.text: That's true. I don't know, you won't be good at it.
jokes.setup: Larry says he couldn't handle an affair
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's blunt agreement
jokes.explanation: Cheryl's deadpan confirmation of Larry's incompetence is both supportive and insulting.

jokes.text: He's been completely snubbing me. It's not even subtle, it's ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Larry notices Rick being cold to him at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid interpretation of professional service
jokes.explanation: Larry's paranoia about the stop-and-chat incident manifesting as perceived persecution.

jokes.text: You gotta stop and talk to people like there's so much information to exchange. 'Hello, how you doing?' 'How are you?' 'Good.' 'How's it going with you?' 'Good.' 'Okay, take it easy.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his aversion to small talk
jokes.punchline: Breaks down the meaningless exchange step by step
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy about the ritual of small talk. Larry's systematic breakdown is both accurate and hilarious.

jokes.text: That's his night? That's why he gets paid, he sits there and waits. It's not a bad job.
jokes.setup: Larry worrying about the limo driver waiting
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's practical but callous perspective on service work
jokes.explanation: Shows class divide - Cheryl's casual dismissal of the driver's experience.

jokes.text: Yeah, I know, and I hated it.
jokes.setup: Cheryl says waiting is the driver's job
jokes.punchline: Larry remembers his own experience driving limo
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's empathy coming from personal experience - he remembers being in service position.

jokes.text: How many times are we gonna talk about Greg?
jokes.setup: Larry keeps bringing up the limo driver
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's exasperation at Larry's obsession
jokes.explanation: Cheryl's frustration with Larry's neurotic fixations during their celebration dinner.

jokes.text: I got some leftovers here. Maybe I'll just get him a doggie bag or something. Is that bad?
jokes.setup: Larry's concern about the hungry driver
jokes.punchline: Larry's plan to give the driver their leftovers
jokes.explanation: Sets up the coming disaster - Larry's good intentions leading to social awkwardness

jokes.text: No, I'm gonna give it to the limo driver.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks if Larry will finish his last shrimp
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued obsession with feeding the driver
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry can't let go of his guilt about the waiting driver.

jokes.text: Just, like, leftovers? There's some shrimp in there and.... There's some rice in there. But I didn't touch any of the shrimp. I divided the rice very carefully. It's all clean, it's all good.
jokes.setup: Larry gives the driver a doggie bag
jokes.punchline: Awkward explanation of the leftover food quality
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe - Larry's well-meaning but patronizing gesture with over-detailed justification.

jokes.text: How am I supposed to eat? There's no fork, there's no napkin. I can't eat this.
jokes.setup: Larry gives Greg leftovers
jokes.punchline: Driver points out the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Sets up the chain of events leading to Larry's arrest - his good intentions create more problems.

jokes.text: Pardon me for suggesting this, but it seems like you're walking out with our silverware.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to bring a fork to the driver
jokes.punchline: Rick's formal accusation of theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Larry's good deed interpreted as theft. Rick's polite formality makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're upset, the other day when I saw you on the street and I didn't do a little stop-and-chat. You know what, I'm not good at artificial small talk.
jokes.setup: Rick accuses Larry of stealing
jokes.punchline: Larry connects it to the stop-and-chat incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing how Larry's social awkwardness compounds into major consequences.

jokes.text: All right, I guess in hindsight I probably should've let the guy eat with his hands. That would've been the better thing to do, right? Just have him get it all over his face and have dogs come up maybe and lick it off him.
jokes.setup: Larry reflecting on his silverware theft arrest
jokes.punchline: Escalates to an absurd image of dogs licking the driver
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry sarcasm escalating to absurdist imagery. Shows his inability to accept he was wrong.

jokes.text: Do you have a tattoo of a star?
jokes.setup: Larry sees the masseuse leaving the courthouse
jokes.punchline: Asks about the psychic's specific detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal - the psychic's wrong details about the affair actually match the masseuse.

jokes.text: How did you know that? Yeah, on my arm, that's wild.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about the star tattoo
jokes.punchline: Masseuse confirms she has it
jokes.explanation: Perfect confirmation that makes the psychic's abilities seem real - she saw the affair with the right person but wrong context.

jokes.text: Yes, Judge Katz. And may I say, 'Shabbat shalom.'
jokes.setup: Larry appears before the judge for sentencing
jokes.punchline: Tries to connect with the Jewish judge
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry attempt to use ethnic solidarity to get out of trouble.

jokes.text: It seems to me there was an indication that you were gonna do what you wanted, when you wanted because you feel because of your celebrity somehow you can do things without consequences.
jokes.setup: Judge considering Larry's sentence
jokes.punchline: Calls out celebrity entitlement
jokes.explanation: Social commentary on celebrity privilege delivered through the justice system.

jokes.text: I could work at the temple on Saturday mornings hand out tallises and yarmulkes.
jokes.setup: Judge asks what punishment would be meaningful
jokes.punchline: Larry suggests community service that's pleasant for him
jokes.explanation: Larry tries to turn punishment into something enjoyable - typical attempt to game the system.

jokes.text: Temple can be quite grueling, as you well know.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests temple work as punishment
jokes.punchline: Pretends temple service is difficult
jokes.explanation: Larry's attempt to make his preferred punishment sound onerous to the judge.

jokes.text: I swear to God, I'm not lying.
jokes.setup: Larry in court-ordered scarlet letter punishment
jokes.punchline: Has to wear a sign about his 'crime'
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual callback to the 'scarlet-letter punishment' from the opening. Larry becomes what he observed.

jokes.text: I didn't beat my wife.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his punishment sign
jokes.punchline: Reveals the sign's accusation
jokes.explanation: Shocking reveal of what the sign says - escalates from silverware theft to domestic violence accusation.

jokes.text: I didn't beat my wife.
jokes.setup: Larry in public wearing sandwich board punishment
jokes.punchline: His desperate clarification to passersby about his actual crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy payoff - Larry's humiliation punishment forcing him to clarify his lesser crime.

jokes.text: That was a water bottle in my pants. A water bottle!
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to explain his situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals another humiliating detail
jokes.explanation: Additional humiliation - suggests his sign also accuses him of inappropriate arousal.

tmdb_overview: Cheryl's psychic busts Larry for a naughty massage, and a restaurant owner busts Larry for stealing forks.
